mnemonics.title: Remember fchdir purpose
mnemonics.content: FCHDIR = File Descriptor CHange DIRectory - changes directory using an open file descriptor instead of a path.

mnemonics.title: Major vs Minor numbers
mnemonics.content: MAJOR = Main driver, MINOR = Minor instance - Think of major as the car model and minor as the specific car number.

acronyms.usage: System call to change working directory using file descriptor
System call to retrieve the current working directory path
System call to create special files (device files, FIFOs)
Field in stat structure containing device ID
Field in stat structure for special file device ID

visualTips.visualization: Think of major number as floor number (which driver) and minor number as room number (which device instance). Major:Minor like 8:0 for /dev/sda (first SCSI disk).
Character = sequential stream (like typing one letter at a time). Block = organized chunks (like loading boxes of fixed size).
fchdir uses an open door (file descriptor), chdir asks for directions (pathname). Open door is faster if you already have it.

analogies.analogy: The working directory is like your current location in a building. When you ask for 'room 5', it's relative to where you are standing.
Device files are like hotel room doors - they provide access to different 'rooms' (devices), each with a unique room number (major:minor).
